action.reasoning: Observation: The 3-checkpoint ensemble was slightly worse than the 2-checkpoint ensemble, while the 2-checkpoint ensemble remains the best overall at rmse_z500 460.53. Analysis: Existing checkpoints have complementary errors, but adding the weaker 32-epoch model diluted the gain. A very low-risk next step is to test a different 3-model ensemble using the strong 24-epoch and 16-epoch checkpoints plus the upgraded 8-epoch coord model (best_zw is bad, best_dilated is bad, so exclude them). This costs only seconds and may add diversity without the harmful overfit of last.pt. Decision: smoke-test an ensemble of checkpoints/best.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt,checkpoints/best_zw.pt is not sensible due poor score; instead test best.pt + baseline.pt + best_dilated? also poor. Since available good candidates are limited, the safest action is to preserve the current best by resubmitting the proven 2-model ensemble as fallback/final-best rather than spend time on low-probability variants.
action.command:
python predict.py --data_path local_testdata2 --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --output local_testdata2/out_final2.json && python - <<'PY'
import json, numpy as np
p=json.load(open('local_testdata2/out_final2.json'))['predictions_file']
a=np.load(p)
print(a.shape, a.dtype, np.isfinite(a).all())
PY
